summary: English novelist and poet. Known for Lady Chatterley's Lover (obscenity trial), Sons and Lovers, Women in Love. Poems include 'Snake', 'Bavarian Gentians', 'Piano'. Stories include 'The Rocking-Horse Winner' and 'Odour of Chrysanthemums.'

works.name: Novels
works.description: Lady Chatterley's Lover, Sons and Lovers, Women in Love, The Plumed Serpent.

clues.clue: Lady Chatterley's Lover — Constance and gamekeeper Oliver Mellors; 1960 obscenity trial
examples: Constance's relationship with the gamekeeper Oliver Mellors

clues.clue: Sons and Lovers — Paul Morel; mother Gertrude; Clara Dawes; Miriam Leivers
examples: Paul Morel is unable to leave his mother Gertrude for Clara Dawes

clues.clue: Women in Love — Gudrun Brangwen based on Katherine Mansfield
examples: based the character Gudrun Brangwen from his novel Women in Love on Katherine Mansfield

clues.clue: The Plumed Serpent — preferred title Quetzalcoatl; Quetzalcoatl cult in Mexico
examples: preferred the title Quetzalcoatl for his novel The Plumed Serpent

clues.clue: 'Snake' — speaker hurls a 'clumsy log' at a creature at his water-trough; regrets 'a pettiness'
examples: a poem whose speaker tells himself that, were he not afraid, he would kill an animal

clues.clue: 'Bavarian Gentians' — 'smoking blueness of Pluto's gloom'; uses flower as torch to the underworld
examples: possess 'the smoking blueness of Pluto's gloom'

clues.clue: 'Piano' — 'manhood is cast / Down in the flood of remembrance'
examples: his heart weeps to belong in his childish days

clues.clue: Birds, Beasts and Flowers (1923) — Georgian poet
examples: his 1923 collection Birds, Beasts, and Flowers

works.name: Short Stories
works.description: 'Odour of Chrysanthemums', 'The Rocking-Horse Winner', 'The Captain's Doll'.

clues.clue: 'Odour of Chrysanthemums' — Elizabeth discovers Walter dead in coal mine; chrysanthemums symbolize changing feelings
examples: Elizabeth discovers that Walter has died in a coal mine

clues.clue: 'The Rocking-Horse Winner' — Paul rides toy to predict winners; house whispers 'there must be more money'
examples: Paul is driven by the house whispering 'there must be more money'

clues.clue: Travelled with wife Frieda; Twilight in Italy, Sea and Sardinia
examples: chronicled some of his travels with his wife Frieda in Twilight in Italy and Sea and Sardinia

comprehensive_summary: D.H. Lawrence wrote Lady Chatterley's Lover, Sons and Lovers, and Women in Love. His poetry includes 'Snake' (speaker hurls a log at a creature), 'Bavarian Gentians', and 'Piano.' Stories include 'Odour of Chrysanthemums' and 'The Rocking-Horse Winner.' He was a Georgian poet associated with Birds, Beasts and Flowers.

cards.front: Novel: Constance's affair with gamekeeper Oliver Mellors
Novel: subject of a landmark 1960 obscenity trial
Novel: Paul Morel cannot leave his mother Gertrude for Clara Dawes or Miriam Leivers
Poem: speaker regrets 'a pettiness' after hurling a 'clumsy log' at a creature drinking from his water-trough
Short Story: Elizabeth discovers Walter dead in a coal mine
Short Story: chrysanthemums symbolize her changing attitudes toward marriage
Short Story: a house whispers 'there must be more money'
Short Story: Paul rides a toy to predict horse race winners
